records.ety: [L. infirmitas : cf. F. infirmite. See Infirm, a.]

defs: The state of being infirm; feebleness; an imperfection or weakness; esp., an unsound, unhealthy, or debilitated state; a disease; a malady; as, infirmity of body or mind.
quotes: 'T is the infirmity of his age.

defs: A personal frailty or failing; foible; eccentricity; a weakness or defect.
quotes: Will you be cured of your infirmity ?
A friend should bear his friend's infirmities.
The house has also its infirmities.
